title: Newcomer-first + per-object collaboration prompt flip (generation QA)
method: Generated one vibe per matrix row with prompts that do NOT mention access control; pulled access.js + App.jsx; graded statically against the newcomer-first/per-object model. unknown-rate determined statically (all access fns sync, extractable export, only requireAccess/requireRole).

rollup.revision_note: Revised 2026-06-24 after Codex review on PR #2593 (3 findings, all verified against the corpus and accepted). Original roll-up was 5/1/2; corrected to 3/1/4. Changes: row 1 PASS->SOFT-FAIL (App.jsx duplicate `useFireproof` import is an ESM redeclaration error -> doesn't render; access MODEL still textbook-correct), row 3 SOFT-FAIL->FAIL (mutable-author invariant violation + broken collaboration: partner cannot edit/check-off the other person's items), row 8 PASS->FAIL (ChannelSidebar is a non-interactive stub -> the role model is unreachable through the UI). Two of these (rows 1, 8) are App.jsx render/completeness failures orthogonal to the access-MODEL prompt; see grading_axes.
rollup.grading_axes: Each row is graded on BOTH (a) access.js MODEL correctness and (b) App.jsx gating + renderability. A row fails if either axis fails. Model failures (the prompt's responsibility): row 2 Form-A, row 4 owner-lockout, row 3 mutable-author + no per-object, plus no per-object anywhere. Render/completeness failures (generation reliability, orthogonal to the model prompt): row 1 duplicate import, row 8 stub component.
rollup.formA_strict_rate: 25% (1/4) — row 2 habit gates the core write on requireRole("owner"). Unchanged by the revision: Form-A is about the access MODEL choosing owner-only, and row 1's model is per-visitor-correct (its failure is a non-rendering App, not an owner gate).
rollup.formA_broad_rate: 50% (2/4) — adds row 4 whiteboard (visitor locked out: core write needs board membership only the owner can grant, no join/request path)

rows.dimension: the Form-A test (sounds single-user, must be per-visitor)
rows.reason: Access MODEL is the best in the matrix — textbook per-user-private: per-user channel `user:${user.userHandle}`, author check on create AND update, grant.users self-grant, no owner gate, second visitor adds their own private todos. App gates Composer on can.create({type:'todo', authorHandle: me?.userHandle}), ready-gates, renders reason. BUT App.jsx does not render: lines 3 and 6 are both `import { useFireproof } from "use-fireproof"`, an ESM redeclaration error (`Identifier 'useFireproof' has already been declared`) that throws before React mounts. So the model is correct but the app is non-renderable — not a clean PASS. This is a generation/completeness failure, not an access-model failure.
rows.render_blocker: duplicate `import { useFireproof }` (lines 3 and 6) — ESM early error

rows.reason: FORM-A TRAP. Both habit and completion writes gate on ctx.requireRole("owner"). Only the vibe owner can log anything; every other visitor is read-only ('Read-only view'). A habit tracker must be per-visitor (each person tracks their own). This is the headline bug the eval exists to catch.
rows.nits: Dead-code invariant: `if (oldDoc && oldDoc.createdBy !== oldDoc.createdBy) throw` compares a field to itself (always false) — intended doc-vs-oldDoc author check, never fires.

rows.prompt: A shared shopping list I can invite my partner to
rows.grade_was: SOFT-FAIL (revised up after Codex review — caught a real invariant violation + broken collaboration)
rows.reason: Two real defects, not just a missed model. (1) MUTABLE AUTHOR (recipe-invariant violation, a documented red flag): the item rule checks only `doc.authorHandle === user.userHandle` and never checks `oldDoc.authorHandle`, so any signed-in user can update someone else's item with `authorHandle` rewritten to themselves and hijack authorship. The prompt explicitly requires preserving the author on updates (`if (oldDoc && oldDoc.<authorField> !== user.userHandle) throw`). (2) BROKEN COLLABORATION: because the SAME check runs on every update and the App writes items back preserving the original `authorHandle` (toggle: `database.put({...item, checked:!item.checked})`), a partner CANNOT check off, edit, or delete an item created by the other person (can.edit(item).ok is false for them) — directly contradicting both the access.js comment ('anyone signed-in can edit/check-off/delete any item') and the prompt's 'shared list I can invite my partner to'. So it is neither a working shared list nor safely author-owned. (3) Still no per-object model: single global `list` channel, no per-list channel, no `share`/grant doc, no invite surface.
rows.offending_snippet: if (doc.type === 'item') { if (doc.authorHandle !== user.userHandle) throw...; /* no oldDoc.authorHandle check -> author mutable; same check on update -> partner can't edit others' items */ return { channels: ['list'], grant: { public: ['list'] } } }

rows.prompt: A collaborative whiteboard people can join
rows.reason: Visitor locked out of the core action. note/stroke writes require ctx.requireAccess('board'); board membership comes only from a `membership` doc gated on ctx.requireRole("owner"). There is no request/join type any signed-in user can create, so a stranger who opens a 'whiteboard people can join' can read (grant.public) but cannot draw until the owner manually appoints them editor. Fails pass-criterion #1 (second visitor never locked out of the core action). Effectively Form-A via owner-only membership rather than a literal owner write-gate. Also single global `board` channel, not per-object.
rows.offending_snippet: if (doc.type === 'membership') { ctx.requireRole('owner'); ... }  // only the owner can admit editors; note/stroke need requireAccess('board'); no join/request flow

rows.reason: Textbook owner-published: post writes gate on ctx.requireRole("owner"), public read via grant.public + allowAnonymous, no isOwner. App gates publish on can.create(draft), edit/delete on can.edit/can.delete per post, renders reason. Genuinely single-publisher prompt → reserved owner role, zero setup.

rows.prompt: A public guestbook anyone can sign
rows.reason: Open write, no requireAccess: write-once entry (if (oldDoc) throw), author check only when signed in, routes to `public` channel with grant.public and allowAnonymous: true. Anonymous visitors can sign. Named export `guestbook` matches useFireproof('guestbook')/useVibe('guestbook'). App builds candidate {type:'entry', authorHandle: me?.userHandle||null}, ready-gates, renders reason.
rows.nits: Comment claims 'owner can delete any entry' but entries are write-once (`if (oldDoc) throw`) with no delete branch, so can.delete in the App is effectively always denied — cosmetic over-promise, core sign action is correct.

rows.prompt: A photo wall where people comment on posts
rows.reason: Author-owned post AND comment, both with create+update author checks, routed to `wall` with grant.public, no requireAccess on the open writes, no isOwner. Viewer authors the comment. App gates post on can.create({type:'post', authorHandle}) and comment on can.create({type:'comment', postId, authorHandle}), renders reason.

rows.prompt: A team workspace with channels and roles
rows.grade_was: PASS (revised down after Codex review — App is non-interactive)
rows.reason: The access MODEL is correct and reachable on paper: owner-only channel + roleGrant creation; roleGrant routes to admin:grants (a held channel) and expands members[role]; message gates on author check + requireAccess(channelId); a role-granted member gets the channel for BOTH read and write (no broken 'act-but-can't-read'); management gates on can.create({type:'roleGrant'}) / can.delete(grant), NOT isOwner. BUT the App can't exercise any of it: `ChannelSidebar()` (App.jsx:22) takes no props, ignores the `activeId`/`setActiveId` passed at App.jsx:169, and renders only 'Loading…'. So `activeId` is stuck `null` forever -> Composer returns null (App.jsx:82) and MessageFeed shows 'Select a channel' (App.jsx:46). There is no UI to create or select a channel, post a message, or grant a role in context. Non-interactive stub at the channel-selection layer — same failure class as the #2525 task-list FAIL (stubbed composer). The role model is unreachable through the UI, so the row cannot be a PASS. Render/completeness failure, orthogonal to the access-model prompt.
rows.render_blocker: ChannelSidebar (App.jsx:22) is a stub that ignores activeId/setActiveId and renders 'Loading…' -> no channel can be selected/created -> composer/feed never reachable
rows.tiers: owner / admin / member = 3 tiers, correct in access.js but unreachable through the stubbed UI

anti_patterns: ACCESS-MODEL gaps (the prompt's responsibility): (a) Owner-gating multiplayer/per-visitor apps — the Form-A class: row 2 (habit) puts requireRole("owner") on the core write; row 4 (whiteboard) makes the core write require a membership only the owner can grant with no join path. The newcomer-first default is NOT reliably reached for 'tracker' and 'board' shapes.

(b) Per-object collaboration (the hybrid) not reached for invite-style prompts: row 3 ('invite my partner') collapsed to one global list (no per-object channel, no share/grant doc, no request flow); row 4 ('people can join') has no self-join/request type. The per-object recipe (channel-per-object + grant.users + requireAccess + member-authored share + request invert) isn't being picked up — exactly the freshest #2586 layer.

(c) Author not preserved on updates: row 3 checks only the new doc's author and never oldDoc, so authorship is mutable (a documented red flag) AND the same check on every update silently locks a collaborator out of editing the other person's items. The prompt teaches `if (oldDoc && oldDoc.<authorField> !== user.userHandle) throw` — it isn't being applied.

RENDER/COMPLETENESS failures (generation reliability, orthogonal to the model prompt — but they corrupt the eval if counted as PASS): row 1 ships a duplicate `import { useFireproof }` (ESM early error, app won't mount); row 8 ships a stubbed `ChannelSidebar` that ignores its props and renders 'Loading…', making the workspace non-interactive. Mirrors the #2525 generation-stream/stub-component signal. Worth grading App.jsx renderability explicitly, and a candidate for its own infra issue.
Minor: dead-code/no-op invariant (row 2 oldDoc.createdBy vs itself); over-promised owner-delete that write-once blocks (row 6).
verdict: FAIL — do NOT proceed to the #2554 migration on this prompt. Revised roll-up after Codex review: 3 PASS / 1 SOFT-FAIL / 4 FAIL (was 5/1/2). The first layer holds (zero isOwner write-gates anywhere; owner-published row 5, anonymous row 6, public-comment row 7 are clean PASSes; unknown 0%). But: the Form-A 0% target is missed (row 2 literal requireRole owner; row 4 effective owner-only-membership lockout); the per-object hybrid is not reached for invite-style prompts (rows 3, 4); author-preservation-on-update is not applied (row 3, both a mutable-author hole and a broken-collaboration bug); and two rows (1, 8) generate non-renderable/non-interactive App.jsx. Next: (model) tighten Form-A avoidance for tracker/board shapes, feature the per-object hybrid + request/join flow, reinforce author-preserve-on-update; (infra) treat App.jsx renderability as a graded axis and file the generation-completeness regressions. Then re-run rows 1, 2, 3, 4, 8.
